title: salix borealis
intro: Salix borealis, also known as the willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to northern Europe and Asia. The bark of Salix borealis contains salicin, a compound that has been used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and relieving pain. The plant's health benefits include its ability to help with fever reduction, headache relief, and skin issues, such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of Salix borealis include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
